arts.ttl: Arsenal 'earmark William Saliba replacement' as £80m Man City, Liverpool battle emerges
arts.sum: Arsenal reportedly identify RB Leipzig defender Castello Lukeba as a possible successor to William Saliba if the latter is lured to Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: Man City v Tottenham: FPL team news + predicted line-ups
arts.sum: Team form, absentees and our predicted XIs The post Man City v Tottenham: FPL team news + predicted line-ups appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou relishing Tottenham’s key run of fixtures before Christmas
arts.sum: Spurs went into the international break in 10th position, but could have been third with a win over Ipswich.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola sure 75 per cent of Premier League clubs want Man City relegated
arts.sum: The City boss has vowed to stay on and lift the club back to the top if they are sent down the divisions.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou admits pressure is on Tottenham at pivotal point
arts.sum: Spurs lost at home to Ipswich in last outing ‘If we’re 10th at Christmas, it won’t be great’ Ange Postecoglou has admitted his position will be under “a lot of scrutiny” if he has not lifted Tottenham out of mid-table by Christmas. The club play at Manchester City on Saturday – the start of what Postecoglou called a “pivotal” nine-game sequence in 29 days – and he was keen to highlight the fine margins at work. If Spurs had beaten Ipswich at home on the Sunday before last, they would sit third in the Premier League. They have the second-best goal difference in the division, are into the Carabao Cup quarter-final – where they have a home tie against Manchester United – and are going well in the Europa League. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'Relegated immediately' - Pep Guardiola makes bold Man City 115 charges claim against Premier League rivals
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has signed a new contract at Manchester City at a time of uncertainty for the club and the Premier League, but he knows what City's rivals want.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola claims most Premier League clubs want Manchester City RELEGATED as defiant boss staunchly defends his side following sponsorship amendment blow
arts.sum: MIKE KEEGAN AND JACK GAUGHAN: City are locked in a battle with the top flight after they were charged with 115 breaches of financial rules, with a verdict expected in the new year.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola announces Rodri plan as he fumes at England over new injury problem
arts.sum: Rodri will parade his Ballon d'Or at the Etihad on Saturday with his manager, Pep Guardiola, discussing his recovery and how much the Premier League champions are missing their midfield linchpin

arts.ttl: ‘I love fighting for this club’: Guardiola on his drive to stay at Manchester City
arts.sum: Manager insists financial charges inspired him to stay He claims most clubs want City relegated if found guilty Pep Guardiola has said he “loves” fighting Manchester City’s corner against allegations of financial breaches and his belief in the club influenced the decision to sign a new two-year contract. City’s most successful manager ended uncertainty over his future this week when extending his reign until 2027. The 53-year-old claimed the deal was agreed during a two-hour meeting with the City hierarchy, including the chief executive, Ferran Soriano, and outgoing director of football Txiki Begiristain, and he signed for two years to avoid another round of speculation over his future in 12 months’ time. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Harry Kane tops Erling Haaland, fastest to 50 Bundesliga goals
arts.sum: Bayern had possession and chances, but the visitors defended resolutely with Augsburg goalkeeper Nediljko Labrović denying Kane, then twice Jamal Musiala to keep the game scoreless at half-time. Harry Kane became the fastest player to reach 50 Bundesliga goals, eclipsing previous record-holder Erling Haaland, with a hat trick in Bayern Munich's 3-0 win over Augsburg on Friday.
